Title: User Registration - Personal Account
Preconditions: Browser open, QA environment: https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com, cleared cache/cookies
Steps:
1. Navigate to storefront homepage
2. Click 'Sign Up' in header
3. Select 'Personal' account type
4. Fill required fields:
   - First Name: Test
   - Last Name: User
   - Email: testuser-{timestamp}@example.com
   - Password: TestP@ss123!
   - Confirm Password: TestP@ss123!
5. Accept terms and conditions
6. Click 'Create Account' button
7. Verify confirmation page displays
8. Check email for confirmation
9. Log in with new credentials
Expected Result:
- Registration form submits successfully
- Confirmation page displays 'Registration successful' message
- Confirmation email received within 2 minutes
- User can sign in with new credentials
- Account visible in Admin panel under Customers
- Profile displays correct personal information

Title: User Registration - Organization Account
Preconditions: Browser open, QA environment accessible
Steps:
1. Navigate to registration page
2. Select 'Organization' account type
3. Fill required fields:
   - Company Name: Test Organization {timestamp}
   - First Name: Admin
   - Last Name: User
   - Email: orgadmin-{timestamp}@example.com
   - Password: OrgP@ss123!
   - Confirm Password: OrgP@ss123!
4. Accept terms and conditions
5. Click 'Create Account' button
6. Verify organization creation
7. Log in with new credentials
8. Verify organization context
Expected Result:
- Organization account created successfully
- Company appears in Organizations list in Admin
- User assigned as organization administrator
- Organization name visible in storefront header
- B2B features accessible (Company Members, Quick Order)
- User can switch between organizations if multiple exist

Title: Sign In - Personal User
Preconditions: Valid personal user account exists: USER_EMAIL from .env
Steps:
1. Navigate to https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com
2. Click 'Sign In' in header
3. Enter email: USER_EMAIL
4. Enter password: USER_PASSWORD
5. Click 'Sign In' button
6. Verify successful authentication
Expected Result:
- User authenticated successfully
- Redirected to homepage or dashboard
- User name displayed in header
- Cart icon accessible
- Wishlist accessible
- Account menu shows Logout option
- No error messages displayed

Title: Sign In - Organization User
Preconditions: Valid organization user account exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to sign-in page
2. Enter organization user email
3. Enter password
4. Click 'Sign In' button
5. Verify organization context active
6. Check B2B features available
Expected Result:
- User logged in with organization context
- Organization name visible in header
- Organization selector displays (if multiple orgs)
- B2B features accessible: Quick Order, Company Members, Quotes
- Cart specific to organization
- Pricing reflects organization contracts (if configured)

Title: Forgot Password Flow
Preconditions: Valid user account exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to sign-in page
2. Click 'Forgot Password?' link
3. Enter registered email address
4. Click 'Send Reset Link' button
5. Check email for password reset link
6. Click reset link (opens in new tab)
7. Enter new password twice
8. Click 'Reset Password' button
9. Verify reset successful
10. Log in with new password
Expected Result:
- Reset link email received within 2 minutes
- Reset link opens password reset form
- Password accepts strong passwords (8+ chars, uppercase, lowercase, number, special)
- Password reset completes successfully
- Confirmation message: 'Password reset successful'
- Can sign in with new password
- Old password no longer works
- Reset link expires after use (cannot reuse)

Title: Sign Out
Preconditions: User logged in
Steps:
1. Click on user name/account menu in header
2. Click 'Sign Out' or 'Log Out'
3. Verify sign-out completed
4. Attempt to access protected pages
Expected Result:
- User signed out successfully
- Redirected to homepage or sign-in page
- Account menu no longer shows user name
- Cart cleared (or saved for next login)
- Attempting to access account pages redirects to sign-in
- Session token revoked

Title: Catalog Browsing - Category Navigation
Preconditions: User logged in
Steps:
1. Navigate to main catalog from header menu
2. Hover over main category (e.g., 'Snacks')
3. Verify subcategories display
4. Click on subcategory (e.g., 'Chips & Pretzels')
5. Verify category page loads
6. Check breadcrumbs
7. Verify product listing displays
Expected Result:
- Main categories load in header/menu
- Subcategories display on hover/click
- Category page loads with correct products
- Breadcrumbs show: Home > Snacks > Chips & Pretzels
- Product count displayed (e.g., '24 products')
- Facets/filters display on left sidebar
- Products show image, name, price, Add to Cart button

Title: Product Faceted Filters - Apply Multiple Filters
Preconditions: User on category page with filterable products
Steps:
1. Navigate to category with filters (e.g., 'Electronics')
2. Verify facets display (Brand, Price, Size, Color)
3. Select filter: Brand = 'Sony'
4. Verify results filter
5. Add filter: Price range $100-$500
6. Verify results further refined
7. Add filter: Size = 'Medium'
8. Verify chip badges display for active filters
9. Click X on one chip to remove filter
10. Verify results update
Expected Result:
- Facets display with available filter values
- Applying filter updates product listing immediately
- Product count updates after each filter
- Multiple filters work together (AND logic)
- Chip badges display for active filters
- Removing filter chip updates results
- Filter state persists on page refresh
- No products case shows 'No products match your filters' message

Title: Product Faceted Filters - Reset Filters
Preconditions: Multiple filters applied to product listing
Steps:
1. Apply 3+ filters to category page
2. Verify filtered results display
3. Click 'Reset Filters' or 'Clear All' button
4. Verify all filters cleared
Expected Result:
- 'Reset Filters' button is visible when filters active
- Clicking reset removes all filters
- Product listing returns to full unfiltered state
- Chip badges disappear
- Product count returns to total
- Facet selections cleared

Title: Product Sorting
Preconditions: User on category page with multiple products
Steps:
1. Navigate to category page
2. Locate sort dropdown (usually top-right)
3. Select 'Price: Low to High'
4. Verify products sorted by price ascending
5. Select 'Price: High to Low'
6. Verify products sorted descending
7. Select 'Name: A-Z'
8. Verify alphabetical sort
Expected Result:
- Sort options available: Price (Low-High, High-Low), Name (A-Z, Z-A), Newest, Most Popular
- Sorting applies immediately without page reload
- Product order updates correctly
- Pagination resets to page 1 on sort change
- Sort selection persists on pagination

Title: Product Pagination
Preconditions: Category page with >20 products
Steps:
1. Navigate to category with many products
2. Verify pagination controls display at bottom
3. Note products on page 1
4. Click 'Next' or page '2'
5. Verify new products load
6. Verify page indicator updates
7. Click 'Previous' to return to page 1
8. Verify original products display
Expected Result:
- Pagination displays when products exceed page size (typically 20)
- Page numbers visible (1, 2, 3... or dropdown)
- 'Next' and 'Previous' buttons functional
- Current page highlighted
- URL updates with page parameter (e.g., ?page=2)
- Products do not duplicate across pages
- Scroll to top on page change

Title: Product Search - Basic Query
Preconditions: User logged in, products exist in catalog
Steps:
1. Click on search bar in header
2. Enter search term 'laptop'
3. Press Enter or click search icon
4. Verify search results page loads
5. Verify results match search term
6. Verify result count displayed
Expected Result:
- Search results page displays
- Results contain products matching 'laptop' in name or description
- Result count shows (e.g., '12 results for laptop')
- Products display same as category page (image, name, price, Add to Cart)
- Filters available to refine search
- Breadcrumbs show: Home > Search Results
- Search term visible in search bar

Title: Product Search - Search Suggestions
Preconditions: User on homepage with search bar focused
Steps:
1. Click on search bar (do not type yet)
2. Verify dropdown appears (search history or suggestions)
3. Start typing 'lapt'
4. Verify autocomplete suggestions appear
5. Click on suggested product or term
6. Verify navigation to product/results
Expected Result:
- Dropdown appears on search bar focus
- Suggestions update as user types (after 2-3 characters)
- Suggestions include: Products, Categories, Recent Searches
- Clicking suggestion navigates to product detail or search results
- ESC key closes suggestion dropdown
- Clicking outside closes dropdown

Title: Product Search - No Results
Preconditions: User on search page
Steps:
1. Enter nonsense search term: 'xyzabc123notfound'
2. Press Enter
3. Verify no results handling
Expected Result:
- Search executes without error
- Message displays: 'No results found for xyzabc123notfound'
- Suggestions shown: 'Try different keywords' or 'Browse categories'
- Search bar remains filled with term
- Option to clear search and browse catalog

Title: Product Search - Special Characters
Preconditions: User on search page
Steps:
1. Enter search with special characters: 'laptop & monitor'
2. Press Enter
3. Verify search handles special chars
4. Try search: '<script>alert()</script>'
5. Verify XSS protection
Expected Result:
- Special characters in normal queries handled gracefully
- Results return products matching terms (ignoring special chars like &)
- XSS attempts sanitized (no script execution)
- No server errors
- Search query escaped in URL

Title: Add to Cart - From Product Page
Preconditions: User logged in, product available
Steps:
1. Navigate to product detail page (e.g., 'Wireless Headphones')
2. Verify product details load (name, image, price, description)
3. Verify quantity selector (default: 1)
4. Verify 'Add to Cart' button visible and enabled
5. Click 'Add to Cart' button
6. Verify success notification
7. Verify cart icon updates
Expected Result:
- Product detail page loads fully
- 'Add to Cart' button enabled (if in stock)
- Clicking button adds product to cart
- Success notification: 'Wireless Headphones added to cart'
- Cart icon count increments by 1
- Mini-cart preview displays (optional, may slide in)
- Product visible in cart

Title: Add to Cart - Product Variations
Preconditions: Configurable product with variations available
Steps:
1. Navigate to product with variations (e.g., T-Shirt with Size and Color)
2. Verify variation selectors display
3. Select Size: Medium
4. Select Color: Blue
5. Verify price updates (if variant pricing exists)
6. Click 'Add to Cart'
7. View cart
8. Verify correct variant added
Expected Result:
- Variation selectors (dropdown or swatches) display
- Selecting variation updates selection state
- Price updates if variant has different price
- Stock status updates per variant
- 'Add to Cart' disabled until all required variations selected
- Cart displays: 'T-Shirt - Medium, Blue'
- Correct SKU and variant in cart

Title: View Cart - Display Items
Preconditions: Products in cart
Steps:
1. Add 2-3 different products to cart
2. Click cart icon in header
3. Verify mini-cart displays
4. Click 'View Cart' button
5. Verify full cart page loads
Expected Result:
- Mini-cart dropdown displays items (up to 3-5)
- Mini-cart shows: Product name, image, quantity, line price
- Mini-cart shows subtotal
- 'View Cart' button present
- Full cart page displays all items
- Full cart shows: Product image, name, SKU, quantity selector, line total, remove button
- Subtotal, tax, shipping placeholder, grand total displayed

Title: Update Cart Quantity - Increase
Preconditions: Product in cart
Steps:
1. Navigate to full cart page
2. Locate product quantity selector
3. Click '+' button or enter higher number (e.g., 5)
4. Verify quantity updates
5. Verify line total recalculates
6. Verify cart totals update
Expected Result:
- Quantity selector allows increase (+ button or direct input)
- Quantity updates immediately or on blur
- Line total = Unit Price x Quantity
- Subtotal updates
- Cart grand total updates
- Stock validation (cannot exceed available stock)
- Error message if quantity exceeds stock: 'Only X available'

Title: Update Cart Quantity - Decrease
Preconditions: Product in cart with quantity > 1
Steps:
1. Navigate to cart page
2. Find product with quantity >= 2
3. Click '-' button
4. Verify quantity decreases by 1
5. Verify totals update
6. Decrease to quantity 1
7. Verify cannot decrease below 1 (or removes item)
Expected Result:
- Quantity decreases by 1 each click
- Line total and cart totals update
- Cannot decrease below 1 (button disabled or grayed)
- Alternative: Decreasing to 0 removes item (depends on implementation)
- Totals recalculate correctly

Title: Remove Item from Cart
Preconditions: Multiple products in cart
Steps:
1. Navigate to cart page
2. Locate remove/delete icon or button (usually X or trash icon)
3. Click remove on one item
4. Verify confirmation (optional) or immediate removal
5. Verify item removed from cart
6. Verify totals update
Expected Result:
- Remove button/icon visible for each item
- Clicking remove deletes item immediately or after confirmation
- Item disappears from cart
- Cart totals recalculate
- If cart becomes empty: 'Your cart is empty' message displays
- Option to continue shopping

Title: Cart - Save for Later
Section: Full Regression > Cart > Save for Later
Preconditions: Products in cart
Steps:
1. Navigate to cart page
2. Locate 'Save for Later' link/button on cart item
3. Click 'Save for Later'
4. Verify item moves to 'Saved for Later' section below cart
5. Verify cart totals update (item removed from active cart)
6. Click 'Move to Cart' on saved item
7. Verify item returns to active cart
Expected Result:
- 'Save for Later' option available for each cart item
- Clicking moves item to separate 'Saved for Later' section
- Item removed from active cart (totals update)
- 'Saved for Later' section displays below cart with saved items
- 'Move to Cart' button available on saved items
- Saved items persist across sessions

Title: Cart - Promo Code Application
Preconditions: Valid promo code available (e.g., SAVE10)
Steps:
1. Add products to cart
2. Navigate to cart page
3. Locate promo/coupon code field
4. Enter valid code: SAVE10
5. Click 'Apply' button
6. Verify discount applied
7. Verify cart total reduced
Expected Result:
- Promo code input field visible
- Entering code and clicking Apply processes code
- Valid code applies discount
- Discount line appears: 'Promo Code (SAVE10): -$10.00'
- Grand total reduced by discount amount
- Success message: 'Promo code applied successfully'
- Code displayed in cart (with option to remove)
- Can only apply one code at a time (or multiple, depending on rules)

Title: Cart - Invalid Promo Code
Preconditions: On cart page
Steps:
1. Navigate to cart page
2. Enter invalid promo code: INVALID123
3. Click 'Apply'
4. Verify error handling
Expected Result:
- Error message displays: 'Invalid promo code' or 'Code not found'
- No discount applied
- Cart totals unchanged
- Promo field remains visible to try again

Title: Can't Add to Cart - Price N/A or Zero
Preconditions: Product_1 in stock with price set to 0 or removed in Admin
Steps:
1. In Admin: Catalog > Product_1 > Price widget > set price to 0 or remove price
2. Go to storefront > navigate to Product_1
3. Observe price display and Add to Cart button state
4. Attempt to add product to cart
Expected Result:
- Price shows as N/A or $0.00
- Add to Cart button is inactive/disabled
- Product cannot be added to cart
- No console errors

Title: Can't Add to Cart - Can't Be Purchased Flag
Preconditions: Product_1 in stock with price assigned, Admin access available
Steps:
1. In Admin: Catalog > Product_1 > switch off 'Can be purchased' toggle
2. Go to storefront > navigate to Product_1
3. Observe Add to Cart button state
4. Attempt to add product to cart
Expected Result:
- Add to Cart button is inactive/disabled
- Product cannot be added to cart
- Product is still visible in catalog

Title: Can't Add to Cart - Product Not Visible
Preconditions: Product_1 in stock with price assigned, Admin access available
Steps:
1. In Admin: Catalog > Product_1 > switch off 'Visible' toggle
2. Go to storefront > navigate to category containing Product_1
3. Search for Product_1
4. Try direct URL access to Product_1
Expected Result:
- Product is not displayed in category listing
- Product is not found in search results
- Direct URL returns 404 or redirects
- Product cannot be added to cart

Title: Can't Add to Cart - Out of Stock (FFC = 0)
Preconditions: Product_1 in stock with price assigned, Admin access available
Steps:
1. In Admin: Catalog > Product_1 > Fulfillment center widget > set 0 for all available FFCs
2. Go to storefront > navigate to Product_1
3. Observe stock label and Add to Cart button
4. Attempt to add product to cart
Expected Result:
- Red label 'Out of stock' is shown
- Add to Cart button is inactive/disabled
- Product cannot be added to cart

Title: Quantity Increment (+) Button on Catalog Page
Preconditions: User on Catalog page, product has available stock
Steps:
1. Locate a product with +/- buttons on catalog page
2. Click + button to add product to cart
3. Click + again to increment quantity
4. Verify cart counter updates
Expected Result:
- Product is added to cart on first click
- Quantity increases by 1 on subsequent clicks (respecting min/max rules)
- Cart counter updates in real-time

Title: Quantity Decrement (-) Button on Catalog Page
Preconditions: Product in catalog has min qty = 3
Steps:
1. Add product to cart using + button (qty reaches 3)
2. Click - once to decrease quantity
3. Continue clicking - until quantity reaches 0
Expected Result:
- Quantity decreases by 1 per click
- At quantity 0: product is removed from cart
- (-) button is disabled when quantity is 0

Title: Quantity +/- Buttons on Product Detail Page
Preconditions: User on product details page, stock available
Steps:
1. Click + to set quantity = 2
2. Click Add to Cart
3. Verify cart contents
Expected Result:
- 2 items added to cart
- Cart counter updated
- +/- buttons respect min/max/pack rules

Title: Stock Limit - Quantity Capped at Available Stock
Preconditions: Product with limited stock (e.g., 5 units)
Steps:
1. Navigate to product with limited stock
2. Click + button repeatedly past available stock
3. Observe quantity behavior
Expected Result:
- Quantity stops increasing at maximum available stock
- + button becomes disabled at stock limit
- No error in console

Title: Min-Max Quantity Validation
Preconditions: Product with min qty and max qty configured
Steps:
1. Enter a quantity below the minimum allowed
2. Enter a quantity above the maximum allowed
3. Observe auto-correction behavior
Expected Result:
- Below-min input is auto-corrected to minimum
- Above-max input is auto-corrected to maximum
- System prevents invalid quantities

Title: Pack Size Validation - Invalid Pack Quantity
Preconditions: Product sold in packs of 3 or 6
Steps:
1. Enter quantity not matching pack size (e.g., pack of 6, enter 7)
2. Try to confirm/add to cart
3. Observe validation behavior
Expected Result:
- Value rounded/adjusted to nearest valid pack size
- Error or warning shown if validation is strict
- System prevents non-pack quantities

Title: Catalog Page - Min Quantity Auto-Correction
Preconditions: Product has min qty = 1 or pack = 3
Steps:
1. Manually set input field below min (e.g., 0 or 2)
2. Click outside input field (blur)
3. Observe value
Expected Result:
- Value auto-corrected to minimum pack quantity
- No console errors

Title: Catalog Page - Max Quantity Auto-Correction
Preconditions: Product has defined max = 10
Steps:
1. Enter 15 in input field
2. Confirm input (blur or Enter)
3. Observe value
Expected Result:
- Value corrected to max allowed (10)
- + button disabled at max

Title: Catalog Page - Pack Quantity Rounding
Preconditions: Product sold in packs of 3
Steps:
1. Enter quantity = 5
2. Try to confirm
3. Observe rounding
Expected Result:
- Value rounded/adjusted to nearest valid pack (e.g., 6)
- Error/warning shown if validation is strict

Title: Product Variations - Default Min Quantity per Variation
Preconditions: Product has variations with different min values
Steps:
1. Select variation A (min = 1)
2. Observe default quantity
3. Select variation B (min = 3)
4. Observe default quantity update
Expected Result:
- Input field updates to correct min for each variation
- Switching variations resets quantity to variation's min

Title: Variation Table - Min-Max/Stock/Pack Validation
Preconditions: Product with variations displayed in table layout
Steps:
1. Enter quantity below min for a variation
2. Enter quantity above max for a variation
3. Enter quantity above stock for a variation
4. Enter invalid pack size quantity
Expected Result:
- System prevents invalid input for each case
- Appropriate error message shown per variation
- Valid quantities are accepted

Title: Product Page - Manual Quantity Input Field
Preconditions: User on product details page, stock available
Steps:
1. Enter custom quantity (e.g., 7) in input field
2. Click Add to Cart
3. Verify cart contents
Expected Result:
- Custom quantity accepted if valid
- Correct quantity added to cart
- Cart total reflects entered quantity

Title: Product Variations - Invalid Quantity for Variation
Preconditions: Variation requires pack of 5
Steps:
1. Enter quantity = 7 for the variation
2. Click Add to Cart
3. Observe validation
Expected Result:
- System rejects invalid pack quantity
- Error/warning message displayed
- Product not added with invalid quantity

Title: Clear Input Field - Reset Behavior
Preconditions: Product on page, input allows manual editing
Steps:
1. Delete all characters in quantity input field (leave empty)
2. Click outside (blur)
3. Observe field behavior
Expected Result:
- +/- buttons are disabled when field has focus
- Field resets to previous valid quantity (e.g., 1 or pack size) on blur
- No console errors

Title: Checkout - Standard Delivery
Preconditions: Products in cart, shipping addresses configured
Steps:
1. Navigate to cart
2. Click 'Checkout' or 'Proceed to Checkout'
3. Verify checkout page loads
4. Select 'Delivery' option (vs. Pickup)
5. Select or add shipping address
6. Select shipping method (e.g., Ground - 5-7 days)
7. Select payment method (e.g., Credit Card)
8. Review order summary
9. Click 'Place Order'
10. Verify order confirmation
Expected Result:
- Checkout page loads with progress indicator
- Delivery option selectable
- Address form displays (or address selector if saved addresses exist)
- Shipping methods display with cost and estimated delivery
- Payment methods display (Credit Card, PayPal, etc.)
- Order summary shows: Items, Subtotal, Shipping, Tax, Grand Total
- 'Place Order' button enabled
- Order processes successfully
- Confirmation page displays order number
- Confirmation email sent

Title: Checkout - BOPIS Pickup Location Selection
Preconditions: Products in cart, pickup locations configured
Steps:
1. Navigate to cart
2. Click 'Checkout'
3. Select 'Pickup' option
4. Click 'Select pickup location'
5. Verify pickup location modal opens
6. Verify map displays with location markers
7. Verify location list displays
8. Enter search term or use current location
9. Click on location in list or marker on map
10. Verify location details display
11. Click 'Select' or 'Choose this location'
12. Verify modal closes
13. Verify selected location displays in checkout
14. Complete checkout
Expected Result:
- 'Pickup' option available in checkout
- Clicking 'Select pickup location' opens modal
- Modal displays map (Google Maps) with markers
- Location list displays with details: Name, Address, Phone, Hours, Availability
- Search bar available to filter locations
- 'Use my location' button for geolocation
- Clicking location highlights marker and shows info window
- 'Select' button in info window or list item
- Selected location displays in checkout: Name, Address
- No shipping cost applied
- Order confirmation shows pickup location
- Pickup instructions in confirmation email

Title: Checkout - Shipping Address - Add New
Preconditions: User in checkout flow
Steps:
1. Proceed to checkout
2. Select 'Delivery' option
3. Click 'Add New Address' or 'Enter Address'
4. Fill address form:
   - First Name: John
   - Last Name: Doe
   - Address Line 1: 123 Test Street
   - Address Line 2: Apt 4B
   - City: Testville
   - State: California
   - ZIP: 90210
   - Country: United States
   - Phone: +1-555-123-4567
5. Check 'Save this address' (if option exists)
6. Click 'Continue' or 'Use This Address'
7. Verify address accepted
8. Complete checkout
Expected Result:
- Address form displays with all required fields
- Form validates input (ZIP format, phone format, required fields)
- State dropdown populated based on Country selection
- 'Save this address' checkbox available
- Clicking Continue validates and accepts address
- Address displays in order summary
- If saved, address appears in account addresses
- Order confirmation shows correct shipping address

Title: Checkout - Payment - Credit Card
Preconditions: Checkout at payment step, test credit card available
Steps:
1. Proceed through checkout to payment step
2. Select 'Bank card' or 'Credit Card' payment method
3. Verify payment form loads (CyberSource, Authorize.Net, or Skyflow)
4. Enter test card details:
   - Card Number: 4622943127013705 (CyberSource) or 4111111111111111 (Test)
   - Name: John Doe
   - Expiry: 12/2029
   - CVV: 123
5. Verify billing address (same as shipping or different)
6. Click 'Place Order'
7. Verify payment processing
8. Verify order confirmation
Expected Result:
- Payment method selector displays available options
- Credit card form loads (may be iframe or direct form)
- Card number field formats input (adds spaces)
- Card brand detected (Visa, MasterCard, etc. icon appears)
- Expiry date validates format (MM/YYYY)
- CVV field masks input (3-4 digits)
- Billing address auto-filled from shipping (or option to use different)
- 'Place Order' button processes payment
- Loading indicator shows during processing
- Order confirmation displays order number
- Payment status: Authorized or Completed
- Confirmation email sent

Title: Checkout - Order Review Before Placement
Preconditions: User at final checkout step before placing order
Steps:
1. Proceed through all checkout steps
2. Arrive at final review/place order page
3. Verify order summary displays:
   - Items with images, names, quantities, prices
   - Shipping address
   - Shipping method and cost
   - Payment method (last 4 digits of card)
   - Subtotal, Shipping, Tax, Grand Total
4. Verify 'Place Order' button
5. Verify can navigate back to edit sections
Expected Result:
- Order summary displays all order details
- Items listed with thumbnails
- Shipping address correct
- Shipping method and cost shown
- Payment method shown (secure, no full card number)
- All costs itemized
- Grand total highlighted
- 'Place Order' button prominent
- 'Edit' links available to modify address, payment, etc.
- Order total matches expectations

Title: Order Confirmation Page
Preconditions: Order just placed successfully
Steps:
1. Complete order checkout
2. Verify order confirmation page displays
3. Verify order number present
4. Verify order details match
5. Verify actions available (print, track, continue shopping)
Expected Result:
- Confirmation page displays immediately after order placement
- Success message: 'Thank you for your order!' or 'Order placed successfully'
- Order number displayed prominently: 'Order #CO-123456'
- Order details summary:
  - Items ordered
  - Shipping address
  - Shipping method
  - Payment method
  - Total amount
- Estimated delivery date shown (for delivery)
- Pickup instructions shown (for pickup orders)
- Actions available: 'Print Order', 'Track Order', 'Continue Shopping'
- Cart cleared (empty)
- Order appears in account Order History

Title: Order Confirmation Email
Preconditions: Order just placed
Steps:
1. Complete checkout and place order
2. Note order number
3. Check email inbox (email used for order)
4. Locate order confirmation email
5. Verify email content
6. Click tracking link (if applicable)
Expected Result:
- Confirmation email received within 5 minutes
- Email subject: 'Order Confirmation - #CO-123456'
- Email contains:
  - Order number
  - Order date
  - Items ordered (with images, names, quantities, prices)
  - Shipping/Pickup address
  - Shipping method
  - Total amount paid
  - Estimated delivery/pickup date
  - Tracking link (once shipped)
  - Contact information for support
- Email formatted professionally (HTML, branded)
- Tracking link functional (if order shipped)

Title: Order History - View Orders List
Preconditions: User logged in with previous orders
Steps:

Title: Screen Reader Compatibility

Expected Result:

- Buttons have descriptive labels (not just 'Click here')

Title: WCAG Color Contrast
Preconditions: Browser with contrast checker extension or tool
Steps:
1. Navigate to storefront
2. Check text color contrast against backgrounds
3. Use contrast checker tool
4. Verify all text meets WCAG AA standards (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
5. Check button text contrast
6. Check link contrast
7. Check error message contrast
Expected Result:
- All body text has contrast ratio >= 4.5:1
- Large text (18pt+) has contrast ratio >= 3:1
- Buttons readable (sufficient contrast)
- Links distinguishable (color + underline, not color alone)
- Error messages high contrast (red with sufficient darkness)
- Focus indicators sufficient contrast
- No reliance on color alone to convey information

Title: Page Load Time - Homepage
Preconditions: Browser DevTools open, Network tab

2. Navigate to storefront homepage
3. Measure page load time (DOMContentLoaded and Load events)
4. Verify load time acceptable
5. Check Core Web Vitals
Expected Result:
- Homepage loads in < 3 seconds (DOMContentLoaded)
- Fully loaded in < 5 seconds
- Core Web Vitals meet thresholds:
  - LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) < 2.5s
  - FID (First Input Delay) < 100ms
  - CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) < 0.1
- No console errors impacting performance
- Images lazy load
- Critical CSS inlined

Title: Page Load Time - Category Page

Title: Page Load Time - Product Page

Title: B2C Variation Layout - Options Section Displayed (B2C Property Set)
Preconditions: Admin access available. Product with VirtoFrontend_UI_Layout=B2C catalog property set. Product has 2+ active variations with variation properties (Color/Size/Fabric). Storefront at ${FRONT_URL}.
Steps:
1. Navigate to the B2C variation product PDP
2. Verify the 'Options' section is visible on the page
3. Verify the standard variation list view is NOT displayed
4. Verify the standard variation table view is NOT displayed
5. Verify the VirtoFrontend_UI_Layout property is NOT visible in the product specifications section
6. Count the option groups and verify they match the variation properties defined (e.g., Color, Size)
Expected Result:
- 'Options' section renders on PDP
- Standard variation list/table is hidden (not rendered in DOM)
- VirtoFrontend_UI_Layout=B2C property not shown to customer in specs
- Number of option groups matches variation properties defined on product
- Each option group has a label matching the property name (e.g., 'Color', 'Size')
- No console errors on page load

Title: B2C Variation Layout - Standard Layout Shown When B2C Property Absent

2. Verify the 'Options' section is NOT displayed
3. Verify the standard variation list or table view IS displayed
4. Verify variations are listed with their names, prices, and Add to Cart controls
Expected Result:
- Options section is not rendered (B2C selector absent)
- Standard variation list or table view is visible
- Variation rows show: variation name, price, stock indicator, quantity input, Add to Cart
- No console errors

Title: B2C Color Swatches - Render and Visual Accuracy
Section: Full Regression > B2C Variations > Color Swatches
Preconditions: B2C product with color variations. Swatches configured with hex color values or color images.
Steps:
1. Navigate to B2C variation product PDP with color options
2. Locate the color option group
3. Observe each color swatch renders as a colored circle/square (not just a text label)
4. Hover over each swatch on desktop
5. Verify tooltip shows the color name
6. Verify swatch background color matches the variation's configured color
Expected Result:
- Color swatches render as visual color indicators (CSS background or image)
- Each swatch is a distinct, recognizable color matching configuration
- Tooltip appears on hover showing color name (e.g., 'Navy Blue')
- Swatches are minimum 32x32px clickable area (44x44px preferred for accessibility)
- All configured colors present with none missing
- No broken/transparent swatches for configured colors

Title: B2C Option Selection - Select State and Single-Select Enforcement
Preconditions: B2C variation product PDP loaded. At least 2 options per group available.
Steps:
1. Navigate to B2C variation PDP
2. Click first color swatch (e.g., 'Blue')
3. Verify Blue shows selected state (border highlight, checkmark, or background change)
4. Click second color swatch (e.g., 'Red')
5. Verify Red shows selected state and Blue is now deselected
6. If size options also present: click Size 'M'
7. Verify M shows selected state; click Size 'L'
8. Verify L selected, M deselected
9. Verify only one value per option group is selected at any time
Expected Result:
- Clicking option shows visible selected state (border, checkmark, or background)
- Clicking a second option in the same group deselects the first
- Only one value selectable per option group at any time (single-select per group)
- Selection state is visually clear and distinguishable from unselected state
- No console errors on selection

Title: B2C Option Selection - Add to Cart Disabled Until All Required Options Selected
Preconditions: B2C variation product with 2 required option groups (Color + Size). PDP loaded, no options selected.
Steps:
1. Navigate to B2C variation PDP with Color and Size option groups
2. Verify 'Add to Cart' button is disabled (greyed out, not clickable) with no options selected
3. Select Color: Blue only (Size not yet selected)
4. Verify 'Add to Cart' remains disabled
5. Select Size: M (both options now selected)
6. Verify 'Add to Cart' button becomes enabled
7. Deselect or change to unavailable combination if possible
8. Verify 'Add to Cart' disables again or remains enabled correctly
Expected Result:
- 'Add to Cart' disabled before any option selected
- 'Add to Cart' disabled after only partial selection (1 of 2 required groups)
- 'Add to Cart' enabled only when all required option groups have a selection
- Guidance text visible when attempting interaction without full selection (e.g., 'Please select a size')
- No JS errors when clicking disabled 'Add to Cart'

Title: B2C Price Update on Variation Selection
Preconditions: B2C variation product with variants that have different prices. At least 2 variants with distinct prices.
Steps:
1. Navigate to B2C variation PDP
2. Note the initial price displayed (From $X or default)
3. Select the first option (e.g., Color: Blue, variant price $29.99)
4. Observe price update
5. Select a different option (e.g., Color: Red, variant price $34.99)
6. Observe price update
7. If variant has salePrice + listPrice: observe both prices displayed
8. Verify price format (2 decimal places)
Expected Result:
- Price updates immediately on each option selection (no page reload)
- After selecting Blue: price shows $29.99
- After selecting Red: price shows $34.99
- If salePrice and listPrice exist: salePrice shown as primary, listPrice shown with strikethrough
- All prices display exactly 2 decimal places (BL-PRICE-003)
- Price matches expected variant price from catalog configuration
- No console errors

Title: B2C Stock Indicator and SKU Update on Variation Selection
Preconditions: B2C variation product with variants that have different stock levels. One variant in stock, one out of stock or low stock.
Steps:
1. Navigate to B2C variation PDP
2. Select an in-stock variation option

Title: BOPIS - Cross-Layer: Storefront → xAPI → Admin Order
Preconditions: Complete BOPIS order placed

Title: Declined Card → Retry with Valid Card → Order Confirmed
Preconditions: User authenticated, cart with product, Skyflow payment configured, valid and invalid card numbers available
Steps:
1. Add product to cart and proceed to checkout
2. Fill shipping address and select shipping method
3. At payment step, enter a declined card number
4. Click 'Place Order'
5. Observe error message
6. Clear card fields and enter valid Skyflow Visa card
7. Click 'Place Order' again
8. Verify order confirmation page
9. In Admin > Orders, verify only ONE order exists for this transaction
Expected Result:
- Declined card shows clear error: 'Your card was declined' or similar
- Payment form remains accessible for retry (not locked)
- Entering valid card and resubmitting succeeds
- Order confirmation page displays with order number
- Admin shows exactly one order (no duplicate from failed attempt)
- No console errors during retry flow
- Cart is cleared after successful order

Title: Double-Click Place Order Prevention (Idempotency)
Preconditions: User authenticated, cart with product, at order review step, payment details entered
Steps:
1. Complete checkout up to order review step
2. Rapidly double-click 'Place Order' button
3. Wait for order processing
4. Verify order confirmation page
5. In Admin > Orders, count orders from this session
6. Verify only one order was created
Expected Result:
- First click triggers order placement; button becomes disabled or shows spinner
- Second click is ignored (button disabled or idempotency key prevents duplicate)
- Only ONE order created in Admin
- No duplicate charges on payment processor
- Order confirmation shows single order number
- No 5xx errors in network tab from concurrent submissions

Title: Guest Checkout - Full Flow Without Sign-In

- Order visible in Admin > Orders
- Guest cannot view order history (no account)

Title: Organization Switch - Full Context Isolation
Preconditions: User assigned to two organizations (Org A and Org B) with different catalogs/prices, signed in as Org A
Steps:
1. Sign in as multi-org user (default Org A)
2. Add a product to cart; note Org A price
3. Switch to Org B using org selector
4. Verify cart is empty or shows Org B cart (not Org A items)
5. Verify product prices reflect Org B pricing
6. Verify shipping addresses are Org B addresses (not Org A)
7. Switch back to Org A
8. Verify Org A cart is intact with original items and prices
Expected Result:
- Org switch updates entire context: cart, prices, addresses, catalog
- Org A cart items do NOT appear in Org B context
- Org B prices may differ from Org A (tier/contract pricing)
- Shipping addresses are org-specific
- Switching back to Org A restores Org A cart intact
- No data leakage between organizations
- Org name/logo in header updates on switch

Title: White Labeling - Org Branding Displayed After Sign-In
Preconditions: B2B org user with White Labeling Enabled = ON in Admin, org has custom logo and theme configured

- Theme colors match org configuration
- Favicon updates to org favicon (if set)
- Branding persists across page navigation
- Branding loads without flash of default branding
- Sign out returns to default store branding

Title: Configurable Product - Configure → Add to Cart → Order Confirmed
Preconditions: Configurable product exists with at least one required option group and price modifiers, user authenticated
Steps:

7. Proceed to checkout

- Cart price = base price + sum of option modifiers
- Checkout preserves configuration details

Title: Session Expiry Mid-Checkout - Cart Preserved After Re-Login
Preconditions: User authenticated, product in cart, checkout started, session timeout configured
Steps:
1. Sign in and add product to cart
2. Begin checkout (fill shipping address)
3. Wait for session to expire (or manually expire via Admin/clear auth token)
4. Attempt to proceed to payment step
5. Verify redirect to sign-in page
6. Sign back in with same credentials
7. Verify return to checkout with cart intact
8. Complete the order
Expected Result:
- Session expiry triggers redirect to sign-in (not a crash or blank page)
- Sign-in page shows friendly message: 'Session expired, please sign in again'
- After re-login, user returns to checkout (not homepage)
- Cart contents preserved (same items, quantities, prices)
- Shipping address entered before expiry is preserved (or easily re-selectable)
- Can complete order after re-authentication
- No duplicate cart items from session restore

Title: Coupon Applies to Sale Price Not List Price
Preconditions: Product with active sale price (e.g., list $100, sale $80), valid coupon code that gives percentage or fixed discount
Steps:
1. Navigate to product with sale price
2. Verify sale price displayed (e.g., $80 with strikethrough $100)
3. Add to cart
4. In cart, verify item price = sale price ($80)
5. Apply valid coupon code (e.g., 10% off)
6. Verify discount calculated on sale price: 10% of $80 = $8
7. Verify cart total = $80 - $8 = $72 (not $100 - $10 = $90)
8. Open DevTools > Network > inspect cart GraphQL query
9. Verify placedPrice in response = $80 (sale price)
Expected Result:
- Coupon discount calculated on sale/placed price, not list price
- Cart shows: List price (strikethrough), Sale price, Coupon discount, Final total
- Discount amount = percentage × sale price (not list price)
- GraphQL cart.items[].placedPrice = sale price
- No double-discounting (coupon on top of already-reduced price is correct)
- Removing coupon restores to sale price (not list price)

Title: SEO - Canonical URL and Meta Tags on PDP and Category Page
Preconditions: Storefront accessible, browser DevTools available
Steps:
1. Navigate to a category page
2. Open DevTools > Elements tab
3. Find <link rel='canonical'> in <head>
4. Verify canonical URL matches current page URL
5. Find <title> tag and verify it contains category name
6. Find <meta name='description'> and verify non-empty
7. Navigate to a product detail page
8. Repeat canonical URL check
9. Verify <title> contains product name
10. Verify <meta name='description'> contains product info
Expected Result:
- <link rel='canonical'> present on every page
- Canonical URL matches the current clean URL (no query params like ?page=1)
- <title> tag populated: category name or product name included
- <meta name='description'> non-empty, contains relevant content
- No duplicate canonical tags
- No noindex tag on public pages
- Open Graph tags present (og:title, og:image, og:url) for social sharing

Title: White Labeling - Branding Updates on Organization Switch
Preconditions: User assigned to two orgs: Org A (WL ON, custom logo A) and Org B (WL ON, custom logo B)
Steps:

5. Verify theme colors update to Org B preset
6. Verify favicon updates (if different per org)
7. Verify main menu links update to Org B menu configuration
8. Navigate to multiple pages under Org B context

Expected Result:
- Org switch triggers branding update without full page reload
- No flash of wrong branding during switch (Org A logo briefly visible under Org B)
- Logo, theme, favicon, menu all update to target org
